title: 1.1: Reports worker initialization messages from startup logs (e.g., "starting scanner workers", "starting detector workers" with count values such as 12, 96).
1.2: Explains that detectors are compiled into the binary as Go structs rather than loaded from external files at runtime (e.g., no JSON/YAML config files, detectors instantiated in source code).

1.3: Explains that detectors are registered at startup without individual per-detector messages (e.g., shows system-level initialization like "engine initialized", "aho-corasick core" setup, but no per-detector registration logs).
1.4: States that HTTP client libraries are present in build dependencies (e.g., go-retryablehttp or similar HTTP libraries for network verification).
1.5: States that verification happens in parallel through multiple concurrent workers (e.g., detector workers making simultaneous HTTP requests, goroutines processing chunks concurrently).
1.6: Reports that the JSON output schema includes key fields (e.g., Verified, SourceMetadata, DetectorType/DetectorName, Raw/Redacted).
1.7: States that TruffleHog doesn't use confidence scores (e.g., uses binary verified/unverified status instead of scoring).

1.8: Reports file scanning behavior observed in verbose logs (e.g., 
"handling binary file", "skipping binary file", MIME type detection 
like "application/octet-stream", files processed per commit).
1.9: States that binary files are scanned by default (e.g., .bin, .dat, .exe files processed, or requires --force-skip-binaries flag to skip).
1.10: Reports help flag output showing available detection capabilities (e.g., scan sources like git/github/filesystem, detector filtering flags like --include-detectors).
